title: Model Mediated Teleoperation with a Hand-Arm Exoskeleton in Long Time Delays Using Reinforcement Learning
affiliations: European Space Agency's (ESA) METERON SUPVIS Justin
abstract: This paper explores teleoperation using a hand-arm exoskeleton in scenarios with long time delays, employing reinforcement learning techniques. The study addresses challenges in bilateral control schemes, haptic feedback, and model-mediated approaches to improve teleoperation performance.
findings: DMPs are effective at adapting to new conditions without uncertainty
Learning from demonstration increases stability and performance of grasping and in-hand manipulation
Various approaches to grasping/manipulation including pre-recorded configurations, deep learning, and deep reinforcement learning
applications: Construction tasks (building a tower with cubes)
